brief_justification: Paper A constructs an exact family of axisymmetric black-hole metrics in general relativity, interpolating between regular spacetimes and the Kerr solution by tuning geometric parameters {M,a} — its machinery is differential-geometric spacetime construction. Paper B is a strongly-indefinite variational/critical-point problem, using minimax levels, spectral-gap and semiconvexity estimates, and the Chambers–Ghoussoub–Bolle deformation theorem to produce infinitely many solutions of a nonlinear Dirac equation via perturbation from symmetry. There is no role-for-role correspondence between metric-interpolation and variational deformation machinery; the only overlap is superficial (radial/axisymmetric setting, the word 'Dirac' vs a GR context).
